subject: Computer Networking
title: Core Web Vitals
description: A short lesson to help you apply Core Web Vitals.

hook.text: Google measures your site's speed with three metrics. They affect your ranking. Meet the Core Web Vitals.

buildup.text: LCP — Largest Contentful Paint. How fast the main content appears. Target: under 2.5 seconds.

discovery.text: INP — Interaction to Next Paint. How fast the page responds to clicks. Target: under 200 milliseconds.

twist.text: CLS — Cumulative Layout Shift. How much the page jumps around while loading. Target: under 0.1.

climax.text: Chrome collects real user data (CrUX). Google uses it for ranking. Lab scores help debug, field data decides rank.

punchline.text: Core Web Vitals: how fast, how responsive, how stable. Three scores that matter.

quiz.question: What does LCP measure?
options: How fast the largest visible content element loads
How many layout shifts occur during page load
How quickly the page responds to user input
